findings.title: Duplicated gap threshold default across two functions
findings.description: The default gap threshold value `5 * 60 * 1000` (5 minutes) is duplicated as a default parameter in both `calculateContinuousWorkDuration` (duration.ts:14) and `buildActivityContext` (activity-context.ts:13). If this threshold needs to change, both call sites must be updated in sync. Since `buildActivityContext` delegates to `calculateContinuousWorkDuration`, the default is effectively defined twice for the same concept.

findings.title: ScreenshotCapture class wraps a single method with no state
findings.description: The `ScreenshotCapture` class holds a single `provider` field and exposes a single `capture()` method that delegates to the provider and adds a timestamp. This is a thin wrapper that could be expressed as a plain function taking the provider as an argument, which would be simpler and more aligned with the functional style used in the rest of the codebase (e.g., `buildActivityContext`, `evaluateTriggers`, `generateResponse` are all plain functions). However, as this is explicitly documented as the Imperative Shell layer, a class may be intentional for future extensibility (e.g., caching, rate-limiting, lifecycle management).

positive_observations: All functions have low cyclomatic complexity (1-4 decision points). The highest is evaluateTriggers at ~4, well within acceptable range.
Maximum nesting depth is 2 levels across all files, far below the 3-level warning threshold.
Every function is short (under 25 lines), well within the 50-line guideline.
Clean use of early returns and guard clauses throughout (e.g., duration.ts:16, activity-context.ts:15, proactive-trigger.ts:18, emotion-state-machine.ts:28).
Named constants used consistently instead of magic numbers (TWO_HOURS_MS, COOLDOWN_MS, DEFAULT_MESSAGE, INITIAL_EMOTION).

Pure functional core (duration, activity-context, emotion-state-machine, response-generator, proactive-trigger) is clearly separated from imperative shell (screenshot.ts, context-engine.ts, persona-engine.ts).
No nested ternaries, no complex conditionals, no deep callback chains.
Parameter counts are 2-4 per function, all within reasonable bounds.
